lede: Wild day in AI land. Sub-second video search just became possible thanks to Gemini's native video embedding, Google shipped Lyria 3 for music generation, and OpenAI launched a safety bug bounty program. Also: a supply chain attack hit LiteLLM this week — Simon digs into the numbers.

quick_links.note: New rules on how your code interactions are handled
quick_links.title: GitHub Copilot data usage policy updates

quick_links.note: Research on running big models in smaller spaces
quick_links.title: TurboQuant: Extreme AI model compression from Google

quick_links.note: Shopping in ChatGPT gets visual and merchant-integrated
quick_links.title: ChatGPT product discovery with Agentic Commerce Protocol

quick_links.note: Open source coding agent in Swift using Claude
quick_links.title: Swift coding agent built from scratch

items.body: Gemini can now natively embed video, and someone immediately built SentrySearch — a tool that searches through video content in under a second. No more scrubbing through footage manually. The repo includes a working demo you can try. HN discussion has people already thinking about use cases from security footage to lecture notes.
items.title: Sub-second video search with Gemini

items.body: Wild experiment from Opper: ask a question and watch 200 different AI models debate it in real-time. Not super practical, but fascinating to see how different models approach the same prompt. Great for understanding model behavior diversity. HN thread here.
items.title: AI Roundtable: 200 models debate your question

items.body: Google's newest music generation model is now available in paid preview through the Gemini API and AI Studio. You can test it today. They're also rolling out Lyria 3 Pro for creating longer tracks in production tools. Finally feels like music generation is getting real developer tooling.
items.title: Google Lyria 3: Music generation API

items.body: The team behind Ente (encrypted photo storage) just shipped a local LLM app. Runs entirely on your machine, no cloud dependencies. Clean interface for chatting with models you download. Worth a look if you're tired of sending everything to APIs. HN discussion.
items.title: Ensu: Local LLM app from Ente

items.body: New framework for building MCP servers (tools that connect AI models to external data/services). Aims to make it faster to ship context providers for Claude and other MCP-compatible models. Early but could be useful if you're building integrations. HN thread.
items.title: FastMCP: Framework for Model Context Protocol servers

items.body: Claude Code now has an auto mode where Claude makes permission decisions on your behalf with safety monitoring. Alternative to the --dangerously-skip-permissions flag. Simon breaks down how it works and the safeguards in place.
items.title: Claude Code gets 'auto mode'

items.body: OpenAI's new bug bounty program focuses on AI-specific risks: agentic vulnerabilities, prompt injection, data exfiltration, and abuse patterns. If you've been poking at model safety, here's your chance to get paid for it. Separate from their existing security bug bounty.
items.title: OpenAI launches Safety Bug Bounty

items.body: The compromised LiteLLM package was live for 46 minutes. Using PyPI download data, researchers estimate 47,000 downloads during that window. Simon's analysis includes the credential-stealing mechanism and which packages were affected. If you use LiteLLM, worth checking. Related: his case for dependency cooldowns.
items.title: LiteLLM supply chain attack aftermath

slack_text:
🌞 *AI Friday Daily Brief — Thursday, March 26, 2026*

7 items today • React in the thread 🔥👎

Wild day in AI land. Sub-second video search just became possible thanks to Gemini's native video embedding, Google shipped Lyria 3 for music generation, and OpenAI launched a safety bug bounty program. Also: a supply chain attack hit LiteLLM this week.

*🔨 Things People Built*
• Sub-second video search with Gemini — search through video in under a second
• AI Roundtable — watch 200 models debate your question

*🛠️ Tools & Releases*
• Google Lyria 3 music generation API now in paid preview
• Ensu — local LLM app from the Ente team, runs entirely on your machine
• FastMCP — framework for building Model Context Protocol servers
• Claude Code gets 'auto mode' for smarter permissions

*🔒 Security & Policy*
• OpenAI launches Safety Bug Bounty for AI-specific vulnerabilities
• LiteLLM supply chain attack: 47,000 downloads in 46 minutes

Full brief with links in thread 👇
